100% coverage achieved — 31 table-driven tests covering all branches: headings, code fences/blocks, frontmatter, HTML comments, bold, italic, precedence rules, edge cases, NoColor mode, full document integration

93.1% coverage achieved (target: 80%+). Added 33 tests for maskCodeBlocks (8), extractCDATASections (5), extractHiddenComments (7), extractYAMLRisks (5), FrontmatterValue (14), extractFrontmatter (6), Format methods (2)

61.2% coverage achieved (target: 60%+). Added 20 tests: expandHome (9), defaultAgentDirs (1), copyFile (3), copyDir (4), verifyAgentDir (4), linkSkill (4), dirExists (3), loadAgentDirs (8), resolvedAgentDirs (1)

17.8% coverage (up from 2.8%). Added tests for padRight (9), maxScrollOffset (8), stripANSI edge cases (OSC/DCS/SOS/PM/APC, standalone ESC, unclosed CSI, multi-byte UTF-8, multiple sequences), truncateLine (4 including ANSI and UTF-8)
Added signal handlers for SIGTERM, SIGHUP, SIGINT in TUI Run() to restore terminal before process exit. Extracted restoreTerminal closure for use by both defer and signal goroutine.
Removed name=path format from loadAgentDirs — now path-only. Updated README config section to show path-only entries. Updated installer test to verify equals format treated as literal path.

Created testdata/{clean,malicious,edge-case}-skill.md fixtures and 4 E2E tests in main_test.go covering: clean skill pipeline (no hidden content), malicious skill detection (frontmatter, HTML comments, CDATA, YAML risks, hidden JS/CSS comments), edge cases (code fence masking, multi-line comments, multi-doc YAML), CLI binary existence check

Added verified agent directory matrix to README with Platform and Verified columns. Goose path confirmed via XDG config convention (~/.config/goose/skills). Pi path noted as best-effort default (~/.pi/skills). Users can override all via config file.

Documented verified agent-directory matrix in README: Claude (~/.claude/skills, confirmed), Goose (~/.config/goose/skills, XDG convention), Pi (~/.pi/skills, best-effort). Added Platform and Verified columns, clarified override behavior.

Expanded Dependency Security section in README with table showing golang.org/x/term and x/sys as sole compile-time deps, clarifying binary is self-contained with no runtime dependencies or third-party modules.
